title: Microsoft Power Automate dropdown column trigger throttling
excerpt: Hello I have a monday board with a dropdown column that I want Users to add multiple labels to, and then have a Power automate flow kickoff and perform some task with all of these labels. My problem is that every time a …

posts.author: scole (Sam)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello I have a monday board with a dropdown column that I want Users to add multiple labels to, and then have a Power automate flow kickoff and perform some task with all of these labels. My problem is that every time a user adds a label the flow will get triggered, so if the user is adding 3 labels at once then the flow gets triggered 3 times, with only the last flow being of any importance. My main concern is that I have other APIs that are getting called in my flow that have limited paid usage. Is there any way of detecting when these triggers happen relative to each other within a power automate flow to cancel the run or maybe waiting until no more changes on that column have occurred for a few seconds? Open to any creative solutions.

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @scole ,

Are you using make.com for this integration?
If the answer is yes, then I think there is an option inside Make to trigger the automation not immediately,but after some time you define, so it will run only once.

Hope this helps,
Oz
